explanation: The primary diagnosis is cervical radiculopathy, with myelopathy documented only as a secondary/incidental finding. Per the explicit NOTE under criterion 2.2, the myelopathy exception applies only when myelopathy is the PRIMARY diagnosis, so the standard conservative treatment requirements apply. The case fails the standard requirements in multiple ways: PT was only 4 weeks (standard requires 6 weeks), and no interventional treatment (2.2(c)) was performed (the waiver only applies under the myelopathy exception pathway, which does not apply here). Therefore Section 2 criteria are not all satisfied, and authorization must be denied.
